text: oh so that's why my components keep re-rendering even when the data looks the same
wait are we talking about the actual lifecycle methods or just the functional hooks now
nobody uses class components unless they're dealing with legacy code that's basically held together by duct tape and prayers
the duct tape is real though
i mean it's just passing data down, why do people make it sound like rocket science
it's fine until you hit prop drilling and then you're stuck passing state through five layers of components just to get it to a button
this is why context exists, but everyone overuses it and kills their performance
the classic trap of putting everything in a global context provider just to avoid typing two extra lines of code
honestly just use composition, it's way cleaner than trying to manage state globally for every tiny thing
